patches.find: The identity θ = −lonE follows from the definition of geographic longitude, not dome physics; fixing V12's broken solar-noon proxy is maintenance, not prediction. WGS84 dependency persists (OPEN-001), and accuracy (7.3%) is 600–700× worse than standard geodesy
patches.replace: θ = −lonE is a relabeling of the centuries-old east-longitude convention — any polar-azimuthal projection of the globe satisfies it automatically. No dome prediction requires it: every distance, bearing, and timing the dome computes from θ is reproduced by standard spherical coordinates using the same WGS-84 longitude input. V13 merely dropped V12's unnecessary solar-noon-UTC indirection. WGS-84 dependency persists (OPEN-001), and accuracy (7.3%) is 600–700× worse than Vincenty geodesy.
patches.rationale: EXP-153: Strengthen WIN-058 finding to explicitly challenge circular angular coordinate relabeling argument. New finding names the relabeling explicitly, states no dome prediction requires it, and preserves WGS-84 accuracy comparison.

patches.find: Tier 3 — Kill-shot binary tests and falsification tests (Parts 5 and 7): Structured tests where the dome and the globe make different, measurable predictions. These are powerful but rely on external datasets (satellite measurements, GPS timing, stellar parallax) that a determined defender could dispute as "measured from the wrong frame." The self-contradiction arguments in Tier 1 face no such objection.
patches.replace: Tier 3 — Kill-shot binary tests and falsification tests (Parts 5 and 7): Structured tests where the dome and the globe make different, measurable predictions, and independent datasets decide between them. Gaia parallax of 1.8 billion stars, the ~38 µs/day atomic-clock correction that makes GPS work, and Southern Hemisphere great-circle distances each yield quantities the dome geometry cannot reproduce without rebuilding its own parameters. These arguments rely on measurements the dome did not choose; the Tier 1 self-contradictions in Parts 2 and 2b go one step further by needing no external data at all, using only the dome's own equations.
patches.rationale: EXP-156: Reframe Tier 3 to lead with strength rather than previewing dome defender's escape hatch. Removes 'that a determined defender could dispute as measured from the wrong frame' clause. Names three concrete kill-shots (Gaia parallax, GPS atomic-clock correction, Southern Hemisphere distances). Preserves Tier 1 primacy in final sentence.
